skills.name: academic-researcher
skills.description: Academic research assistant for literature reviews, paper analysis, citation formatting, and scholarly writing.

skills.name: commit
skills.description: Execute git commits using the Conventional Commits specification with intelligent staging and message generation.

skills.name: data-viz
skills.description: Guide for publication-quality figures in R (ggplot2), Python (matplotlib/seaborn), and Stata. Covers chart type selection, color, axes, error bars, journal formatting, event study plots, coefficient plots, and export settings.

skills.name: debug
skills.description: Systematic debugging protocol: reproduce, isolate, hypothesize, verify, fix. Covers error message interpretation, common bug patterns, and debugging tools for Python, R, Stata, and Bash.

skills.name: docx
skills.description: Skill for reading, editing, and manipulating Word (.docx) documents, including tracked changes and comments.

skills.name: empirical-workflow
skills.description: Scaffold and guide empirical economics projects: project structure, data cleaning checklists, regression specification, table shells, and robustness checklists. Covers Stata, R, and Python.

skills.name: new-project
skills.description: Scaffold a new empirical research project with a standard directory structure, a CLAUDE.md copied from the permanent template, and a generated README.md.

skills.name: pdf
skills.description: Skill for working with PDF files: reading, form filling, annotation, and conversion.

skills.name: pptx
skills.description: Skill for creating and editing PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations programmatically.

skills.name: presentation-generator
skills.description: Generate academic Beamer slide decks from existing content with multi-agent review. Covers creating or restructuring LaTeX/Beamer presentations, integrating R/Python/Stata figures, and the iterative compile-review-fix workflow.

skills.name: presentation-rhetoric
skills.description: Framework for the rhetoric, narrative structure, and visual grammar of effective slide decks. Covers argument structure, cognitive load, assertion titles, visual hierarchy, and audience persuasion in academic or professional contexts.

skills.name: prose-editor
skills.description: Actively rewrite and improve prose for clarity, economy, and precision. Produces revised text, not just critique. Covers academic papers, introductions, abstracts, emails, and reports.

skills.name: referee-report
skills.description: Structured workflow for writing peer review reports for academic economics journals: reading protocol, evaluation dimensions, report structure, and language guidance.

skills.name: session-end
skills.description: Close a Claude Code session by documenting what was accomplished, updating CLAUDE.md with the current project structure, a session log entry, and a forward-looking todo list.

skills.name: session-start
skills.description: Open a Claude Code session by reading all relevant .md files and reconstructing project context from CLAUDE.md, README.md, memory files, and recent git history.

skills.name: split-pdf
skills.description: Deep-read an academic paper by splitting it into 4-page chunks and extracting structured notes across 8 dimensions: research question, audience, method, data, statistical methods, findings, contributions, and replication feasibility.

skills.name: technical-presentation
skills.description: Framework for creating and delivering effective technical presentations, demos, and talks.

skills.name: xlsx
skills.description: Skill for reading, writing, and manipulating Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheets.
